items.title: How a Brute Force Attack Unmasked a Ransomware Infrastructure Network
items.description: A routine RDP brute-force alert led to unusual credential hunting and a geo-distributed VPN-linked infrastructure. Huntress Labs explains how one compromised login unraveled a suspected ransomware-as-a-service ecosystem tied to initial access brokers. [...]

items.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Uses 23 Exploits Across Five Chains Targeting iOS 13–17.2.1
items.description: Google said it identified a "new and powerful" exploit kit dubbed Coruna (aka CryptoWaters) targeting Apple iPhone models running iOS versions between 13.0 and 17.2.1. The exploit kit featured five full iOS exploit chains and a total of 23 exploits, Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) said. It's

items.title: How Pirated Software Turns Helpful Employees Into Malware Delivery Agents
items.description: Employees seeking free versions of paid software may unknowingly install malware-laced “cracked” apps that can steal credentials, deploy cryptominers, or open the door to ransomware. The post How Pirated Software Turns Helpful Employees Into Malware Delivery Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical FreeScout Vulnerability Leads to Full Server Compromise
items.description: A patch bypass for an authenticated code execution bug, the flaw leads to zero-click remote code execution attacks. The post Critical FreeScout Vulnerability Leads to Full Server Compromise appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: APT41-Linked Silver Dragon Targets Governments Using Cobalt Strike and Google Drive C2
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an advanced persistent threat (APT) group dubbed Silver Dragon that has been linked to cyber attacks targeting entities in Europe and Southeast Asia since at least mid-2024. "Silver Dragon gains its initial access by exploiting public-facing intern

items.title: VMware Aria Operations Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
items.description: The recently patched CVE-2026-22719 can be exploited by an unauthenticated attacker for remote code execution. The post VMware Aria Operations Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited VMware Aria Operations Flaw CVE-2026-22719 to KEV Catalog
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting Broadcom VMware Aria Operations to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing active exploitation in the wild. The high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-22719

items.title: CISA flags VMware Aria Operations RCE flaw as exploited in attacks
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a VMware Aria Operations vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-22719 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, flagging the flaw as exploited in attacks. [...]

items.title: Paint maker giant AkzoNobel confirms cyberattack on U.S. site
items.description: The multinational Dutch paint company AkzoNobel has confirmed to BleepingComputer that hackers breached the network of one of its U.S. sites. [...]

items.title: Microsoft: Hackers abuse OAuth error flows to spread malware
items.description: Hackers are abusing the legitimate OAuth redirection mechanism to bypass phishing protections in email and browsers to take users to malicious pages. [...]

items.title: LexisNexis confirms data breach as hackers leak stolen files
items.description: American data analytics company LexisNexis Legal & Professional has confirmed to BleepingComputer that hackers breached its servers and accessed some customer and business information. [...]

items.title: Compromised Site Management Panels are a Hot Item in Cybercrime Markets
items.description: Compromised cPanel credentials are being sold in bulk across underground channels as plug-and-play phishing and scam infrastructure. Flare explains how analyzing 200,000 underground posts reveals a commoditized market for hacked site management panels. [...]

items.title: AI Agents: The Next Wave Identity Dark Matter - Powerful, Invisible, and Unmanaged
items.description: The Rise of MCPs in the Enterprise The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming a practical way to push LLMs from “chat” into real work. items.title: Star Citizen game dev discloses breach affecting user data
items.description: Cloud Imperium Games (CIG), the game developer behind Star Citizen and Squadron 42, says attackers breached systems containing some users' personal information in January. [...]

items.title: UH Cancer Center data breach affects nearly 1.2 million people
items.description: The University of Hawaii confirmed that a ransomware gang stole the data of nearly 1.2 million individuals in August 2025 after breaching its Cancer Center's Epidemiology Division. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Warns OAuth Redirect Abuse Delivers Malware to Government Targets
items.description: Microsoft on Monday warned of phishing campaigns that employ phishing emails and OAuth URL redirection mechanisms to bypass conventional phishing defenses implemented in email and browsers. The activity, the company said, targets government and public-sector organizations with the end goal of redire

items.title: Android gets patches for Qualcomm zero-day exploited in attacks
items.description: Google has released security updates to patch 129 Android security vulnerabilities, including an actively exploited zero-day flaw in a Qualcomm display component. [...]

items.title: Google Confirms CVE-2026-21385 in Qualcomm Android Component Exploited
items.description: Google on Monday disclosed that a high-severity security flaw impacting an open-source Qualcomm component used in Android devices has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-21385 (CVSS score: 7.8), a buffer over-read in the Graphics component. "Memory corruption when a

items.title: CyberStrikeAI tool adopted by hackers for AI-powered attacks
items.description: Researchers warn that a newly identified open-source AI security testing platform called CyberStrikeAI was used by the same threat actor behind a recent campaign that breached hundreds of Fortinet FortiGate firewalls. [...]

items.title: New Chrome Vulnerability Let Malicious Extensions Escalate Privileges via Gemini Panel
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched security flaw in Google Chrome that could have permitted attackers to escalate privileges and gain access to local files on the system.
